mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is particularly useful for ensuring compliance and security in CI/CD pipelines. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resources and potential performance impacts during scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to initiate vulnerability scanning.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    kind: Pod
    name: example-pod
EOF

```

> Creates a sample VulnerabilityReport CR.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    kind: Pod
    name: example-pod
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is processed correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreports -n default

```

> Lists the VulnerabilityReports in the 'default' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all vulnerability reports and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current state of Trivy Operator pods.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Trivy Operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "VulnerabilityReport" in version "trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for VulnerabilityReport may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Trivy Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be experiencing high load or resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and consider scaling the operator or increasing node resources.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or lacking necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the operator's logs for errors and ensure it has the required RBAC permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Vulnerability reports are not generated.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may not be watching the correct resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the configuration of the Custom Resource and ensure it targets the correct Kubernetes resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes resource types and improved scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    kind: Pod
    name: example-pod
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
